qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded multipart form-data processing enables denial-of-service on unauthenticated webhook endpoints
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable path: this finding concerns unbounded multipart parsing on unauthenticated webhook endpoints, while the advisory concerns authenticated credential theft through name-based credential resolution and generic HTTP credential permission-checker bypass in Community Edition.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described credential-theft root cause: a member user can resolve another user's generic HTTP credential by name and bypass pre-execution permission checks for httpBasicAuth, httpHeaderAuth, or httpQueryAuth credentials.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-m63j-689w-3j35 describes n8n Community Edition credential theft by an authenticated global:member user through chained authorization flaws in the credential pipeline.

The advisory root cause is name-based credential resolution without ownership or project-scope enforcement combined with generic HTTP credential types being skipped by the credentials permission checker during workflow execution validation.

The only qualifying finding in this run, unbounded-multipart-webhook-dos, reports formidable multipart processing on unauthenticated webhook, form, webhook-test, form-test, and MCP endpoints with missing maxFields, maxFiles, maxFieldsSize, and total request-size limits.

The finding does not identify credential lookup, credential ownership checks, project-scope validation, generic HTTP credential pre-execution validation, or unauthorized decryption/use of another user's credential.
